@micahstubbs On the other hand, in a society where our biggest companies are tech companies, people who choose the corner radii for the buttons we press on our phones are perceived as very high value for arguably low value tasks to society at large- some get equity.

@micahstubbs If our garbage stopped being picked up, Ubers stopped picking people up, our streets stopped being paved, our articles stopped being updated, it would have tremendous consequences. However, the people that perform these tasks are still somehow seen as low value.

@micahstubbs We are data janitors, information librarians, in a society that bases value on how much revenue you generate, meaningful tasks with low monetary value bleed into perceived low societal value and thus low respect

Looking at the total number of removals, there are counties across the US who are removing hundreds of thousands of registered voters. 

Maricopa County, AZ removed 223K voter registrations
Los Angeles County, CA removed 168K
Broward County, FL removed 123K https://t.co/BKos2EweTP

When you map voter removals per registered voter on a county level across the US, Indiana glows red hot, with many counties removing one voter registration for every 4 registered voters. https://t.co/41he0PR0Ak
@Its_Katka Or was totally unaware about the #StuffOrEats hashtag until I showed it to him over the weekend and he was a bit appalled frankly

When looking at voters removed / registered voters and split into 8 Jenks breaks...

3 counties in the top break 0.48-0.87

Warren County, IL: 11,814 registered voters - 8,140 removed

Jersey County, IL: 16,341 RV - 14,179 removed

Monroe County, IN: 94,206 RV - 45,086 removed https://t.co/GihFNWEEPf
